sources.title: Bessemer State of the Cloud 2024 - Revenue Models
SaaStr: Forecasting Revenue with Usage-Based Pricing

result.summary: Usage-based pricing companies require fundamentally different forecasting models than seat-based SaaS, using cohort-based usage expansion curves and commitment-vs-overage analysis, with best-in-class companies achieving 120-150% net dollar retention through natural usage growth.
key_findings: Cohort-based forecasting using 6-month trailing usage growth per customer segment is 3x more accurate than top-down ARR projections for usage-based models
Committed usage contracts with overage billing yield the most predictable revenue with 85-90% of revenue from commitments and 10-15% overage upside
Revenue volatility is 2.5x higher for pure consumption models vs hybrid (commit + usage), requiring larger cash reserves and making quarterly guidance challenging for public companies
